items.text: 1. Per-stage verdict in retro.json stage_verdicts: discovery (001), planning (002), execution (E1-E4 / iters 003-006), closeout (007) - did each buy back its ceremony cost? (Evidence: retro.json validates; stage_verdicts present.)

2. Objective done-when assessed clause by clause in retro.json done_when (MCP principles distilled into a checklist; CLI assessed; gaps that matter closed - verb discovery, structured --json output, structured errors; no new dependency; no LLM), each MET/NOT with evidence. (Evidence: retro.json done_when.)

3. Overall verdict + carry-forwards in retro.json (incl. deferred grammar standardisation P4 and any agent-ergonomics follow-ons). retro authored via `lume retro`. (Evidence: retro.json present + consistent.)
4. Workstream closed: closeout iteration accepted, then `lume close`; objective.json status flips to closed. Full suite green. (Evidence: `lume status` shows agent-friendly-cli closed; tests pass.)

dod.preamble: Close-out for agent-friendly-cli. Assess whether distilling MCP's principles and applying the adopted subset (verb discovery, --json structured output, structured errors, per-verb input schema) to the CLI bought back its cost; judge the objective's done-when clause by clause; record carry-forwards (notably the deferred grammar cleanup); and close on the engine's own verbs. Authored as data (retro.json).

handback:
E5 closeout complete. agent-friendly-cli ready to close.

- retro.json authored (4 stage verdicts, 4 done-when clauses all MET, overall verdict, 4 carry-forwards) via lume retro.
- 291 tests pass.

All adopted MCP-informed principles shipped: verb discovery, --json structured output across read + write, structured errors, per-verb input schema; grammar documented; protocol machinery rejected (stdlib-only, deterministic). Accept, then lume close.
self_review:
All 4 DoD items complete; 291 tests pass; retro.json authored via lume retro.

Per-stage verdict + done-when in retro.json - all four done-when clauses MET: MCP principles distilled to a checklist (discovery.json), CLI audited, the gaps that matter closed (P1 verb discovery, P2 input schema, P5 --json output, P6 structured errors; P3 pre-existing; P4 documented), with no new dependency and no LLM. Overall: net positive - MCP as teacher not dependency; an agent can self-orient end to end via stdlib-only verbs.

Carry-forwards recorded: deferred grammar standardisation (P4); a GitHub TrackingStore as the next backing follow-on; hand-authored catalog inputs could later derive from the parser; and the backtick-in-decide process lesson.

Closing in order: handback -> accept -> lume close.
